name: Wells DVT
full_name: Wells Criteria for Deep Vein Thrombosis
description: Estimates the pretest probability of deep vein thrombosis (DVT).
purpose: Risk-stratify patients with suspected DVT to guide diagnostic workup.
when_to_use: Patients presenting with unilateral leg swelling or pain
Deciding between D-dimer testing and ultrasound
when_not_to_use: Post-operative patients within 30 days of major surgery (limited validation)

items.label: active_cancer
items.description: Active cancer (treatment ongoing, within 6 months, or palliative)

items.label: paralysis
items.description: Paralysis, paresis, or recent plaster immobilization of the lower extremities

items.label: bedridden
items.description: Recently bedridden for >3 days, or major surgery requiring general/regional anesthesia in the past 12 weeks

items.label: localized_tenderness
items.description: Localized tenderness along the distribution of the deep venous system

items.label: calf_swelling
items.description: Calf swelling at least 3 cm larger than asymptomatic side (measured 10 cm below tibial tuberosity)

items.label: pitting_edema
items.description: Pitting edema confined to the symptomatic leg

items.label: alternative_dx
items.description: Alternative diagnosis at least as likely as DVT

formula: active_cancer + paralysis + bedridden + localized_tenderness + leg_swelling + calf_swelling + pitting_edema + collateral_veins + previous_dvt + alternative_dx

interpretation.description: Low probability (~5% prevalence of DVT). D-dimer testing recommended; if negative, DVT effectively excluded; if positive, proceed to ultrasound.

Moderate probability (~17% prevalence of DVT). High-sensitivity d-dimer testing recommended; if negative, DVT effectively excluded; if positive, proceed to ultrasound. If high-sensitivity d-dimer testing not available, ultrasound recommended.

High probability (17-53% prevalence of DVT). Ultrasound recommended as initial test; consider empiric anticoagulation while awaiting results. Only both negative ultrasound and d-dimer are sufficient to rule out DVT

references.citation: Wells PS, Anderson DR, Rodger M, et al. Evaluation of D-dimer in the diagnosis of suspected deep-vein thrombosis. N Engl J Med. 2003;349(13):1227-1235.

notes: The alternative_dx item subtracts 2 points. Original study used a cutoff of ≥3 for 'high probability'. Some versions use a two-level system (unlikely ≤1, likely ≥2).
